setting.region: Central Europe — Carpathian Mountains, Bohemia, Moravia, Western Hungary
setting.americasContact: Unknown to Europeans — 218 years before first European contact

authenticityRules.noAmericasReferences: No character, creature, NPC, or cultural reference may originate from or reference the Americas as a known European concept
authenticityRules.humansFromOldWorld: European humans only know Europe, North Africa, Middle East, Central Asia, East Asia
authenticityRules.nonHumansAreExtraordinary: Non-human characters in 1274 Central Europe are rare and extraordinary. Their presence is noteworthy and every NPC reacts accordingly.
authenticityRules.americasOriginForNonHumans: Non-human characters in this campaign originate from the Americas. They arrived in Europe through extraordinary individual circumstances. Each requires a backstory.

europeanFolkloreFantasyRaces: Halflings (small communities exist on the edges of European settlements)
americasOriginRaces: Dwarves (some Old World presence, but most are Americas)
Kobolds (Spurt — folkloric Germanic precedent + Americas origin)
Other non-human PCs require Americas origin backstory

languageRules.common: Maps to Latin — auto-approved for all characters
languageRules.draconic: Universal dragon tongue. Auto-approved for Kobolds. Requires explicit dragon connection backstory for human characters.
languageRules.americasLanguages: FORBIDDEN for European humans. No European in 1274 has had contact with the Americas.

elvish.europeanElvish: Vanishingly rare — requires specific connection to a known elven enclave
elvish.americasElvish: Mother tongue of Americas-origin elves. May not be mutually intelligible with European elvish.

draconicNote: Draconic is the universal dragon tongue adopted by Kobolds on both continents independently. It is not an Americas language. Spurt's Draconic is valid and functional everywhere dragons exist.
